topic: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
reasoning: NASA astronauts have confirmed the Great Wall is not visible with the naked eye from space; it's too thin relative to orbital altitude.

topic: Humans use only 10% of their brains.
reasoning: Neuroimaging studies show humans use virtually all of their brain; even 'unused' regions have metabolic activity.

topic: Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short for his era.
reasoning: Napoleon was 5'7" (170 cm), which was approximately average for men in early 1800s France. The 'short' claim comes from British propaganda.

topic: Marie Curie was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize.
reasoning: Marie Curie won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903 (shared with Pierre Curie and Henri Becquerel), making her the first woman laureate.

topic: The Amazon rainforest produces approximately 20% of the world's oxygen (net).
reasoning: While the Amazon produces ~6-9% of oxygen, it also consumes ~6-9%, making net production near zero. The '20%' figure is a persistent myth.

topic: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
reasoning: Lightning frequently strikes the same location multiple times; tall structures like the Empire State Building are struck ~20 times per year.

topic: Einstein failed mathematics as a child.
reasoning: Einstein was actually a strong student in mathematics; he scored high marks in arithmetic and algebra. This myth likely arose from confusion with his early physics struggles.

topic: Thomas Edison solely invented the practical lightbulb.
reasoning: Edison improved and commercialized the lightbulb, but Hiram Maxim, Humphry Davy, and Warren de la Rue contributed earlier foundational work.

topic: The Great Fire of London (1666) destroyed most of the city.
reasoning: The fire destroyed approximately 85% of London's housing and 13,200 buildings, including most of the Medieval City of London.
Watson and Crick discovered the double-helix structure of DNA, but Rosalind Franklin and Maurice Wilkins' crystallography work was essential; DNA itself was discovered earlier.
